channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Upcoming Events and Activities in Ottawa: April 10–12
item.description: This weekend in Ottawa offers a variety of activities and local news highlights. Residents and visitors can enjoy cultural events, sports, and community happenings across the city. Among key updates, ...

item.title: Kenya MPs Urge Stronger Laws and Police Accountability in War Against Drug Abuse
item.description: A parliamentary committee in Kenya has raised concerns over the failure to effectively address alcohol and drug abuse in the country. In a meeting with the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI)...

item.title: Johannesburg Court Ruling Alters How Sectional Title Levies Are Recovered
item.description: A recent ruling by the Johannesburg High Court has significantly changed the way levy arrears are recovered in sectional title schemes. In the case of Centenario Body Corporate v Thandeka Mlotya, the ...

item.title: Landco Resorts Warn Public About Fake Booking Pages and Coordinate with NBI
item.description: Landco Lifestyle Ventures, the developer behind the beach destinations CaSoBe in Calatagan, Club Laiya in Batangas, and Costa Azalea in Davao, has issued a warning against fraudulent social media page...

item.title: Challenges and setbacks in the UK's military transformation plans
item.description: The UK government's ongoing push to overhaul its military strategy has encountered numerous obstacles, sparking significant criticism. Experts argue that the intended transformation, aimed at modernis...

item.title: Dallas Seeks to Expand Financial Sector by Attracting Banks from New York
item.description: Dallas is making a concerted push to establish itself as a major financial hub, aiming to rival New York. The city has attracted significant investment from major banks, including Goldman Sachs, which...

item.title: Child tragically dies following suspected dog attack in Redcar
item.description: A child has tragically died in a suspected dog attack in Redcar, Cleveland, after police were called to an address on Hardale Grove in Dormanstown. Armed officers arrived shortly after 13:30 BST follo...

item.title: Key Art Trends Shaping Collectors’ Choices in 2026
item.description: In 2026, the art market is evolving with trends that reflect both the cultural moment and the changing preferences of collectors. Smaller artworks are increasingly popular, driven by the rise of open-...

item.title: Arbitrator finds Okanagan College violated faculty agreement in 2025 layoffs
item.description: An arbitrator has ruled that Okanagan College breached its collective agreement with faculty when it laid off staff in 2025, prompting relief among affected employees and their union. The interim deci...

item.title: Trump tasks JD Vance with finding a way to resolve the US-Iran conflict as peace talks with Pakistan approach
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for peace talks aimed at finding a resolution to the escalating conflict between the US and Iran. Vance, known for his scepticism towards foreig...

item.title: Group of citizens and lawyers asks Supreme Court to halt Sara Duterte impeachment process
item.description: Seventeen individuals, consisting of lawyers and private citizens, have filed a petition with the Supreme Court of the Philippines seeking to join the case that aims to stop the impeachment proceeding...

item.title: Ogun State renames Sagamu–Iperu–Sapade Expressway after President Tinubu and announces new infrastructure projects
item.description: The Ogun State Government don officially rename the Sagamu–Iperu–Sapade Expressway as the Bola Ahmed Tinubu Expressway to honour the President's contributions to Nigeria and his local roots for Iperu....

item.title: AFP Supports US-Backed Fuel Depot Proposal in Mindanao to Strengthen Defense
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) has expressed support for a proposed fuel depot in Mindanao that would be backed by the United States, emphasizing its role in strengthening the country's def...

item.title: Ijaw and Ogoni Youths Demand Inclusion and Local Control Over Pipeline Surveillance in Niger Delta
item.description: Youth groups in Rivers State, including the Ijaw Youth Council (IYC) and National Youth Council of Ogoni People (NYCOP), have protested their alleged exclusion from a National Assembly roundtable on p...

item.title: Israel’s Assault on Lebanon Threatens US-Iran Peace Efforts
item.description: The editorial discusses the worsening situation in Lebanon following Israel’s recent attacks that coincided with a two-week truce between the United States and Iran, brokered by Pakistan. Over 200 peo...

item.title: Rebel Wilson's 'The Deb': A Warm Comedy, But the Musical Element Falls Flat
item.description: Rebel Wilson's directorial debut, 'The Deb,' is finally hitting cinemas after a tumultuous journey marked by legal troubles and delays. The film, a comedy set in a drought-stricken Australian town cal...

item.title: Court Orders Equal Division of Matrimonial Property After Asset Transfer Dispute
item.description: A Nairobi High Court has ruled that a man, codenamed IMW, unlawfully tried to shield family assets from his estranged wife, Ms CMP, during their divorce proceedings. The court uncovered a scheme invol...

item.title: Baobab Microfinance Bank Opens New Head Office in Lagos to Improve Services
item.description: Baobab Microfinance Bank don officially move their Head Office go No. 129 Ikorodu Road, Lagos. This move no be just for change building but e dey show say the bank dey grow and dey ready to serve thei...

item.title: Péter Magyar: The Former Orbán Loyalist Leading Hungary's Opposition Movement
item.description: Péter Magyar, once a loyalist of Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party, has rapidly emerged as a significant figure in Hungarian politics. His opposition movement, centred around the Tisza party, has gained con...

item.title: PM Shehbaz and Bilawal discuss national unity and Pakistan’s mediation in US-Iran peace efforts
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari held a telephone conversation on Thursday where both leaders agreed that national harmony was essential t...
